# Invoice Module LLM Guide

Purpose: invoices for Cloudflare Workers + D1. Completes booking/order -> payment
-> invoice; powers client-portal billing.

Entry points:
- `src/index.ts` exports the safe public surface.
- `src/use-cases/` holds framework-neutral behavior: createInvoice, addLineItem,
  issueInvoice, recordPayment, voidInvoice, listInvoices, dueForReminder.
- `src/totals.ts` is the pure money math (integer cents, per-line tax rounding).
- `src/ports/` defines InvoiceStore and NumberAllocator.
- `src/adapters/` contains D1 + memory invoice stores and atomic + memory allocators.
- `migrations/0001_invoice.sql` owns invoices, invoice_line_items,
  invoice_sequences (atomic counter), invoice_payments (idempotency ledger).
- `module.json` is the machine-readable manifest.

Model:
- Core record module. Lifecycle: draft -> open (issued, numbered, frozen) -> paid | void.
- Money is integer cents; tax is in basis points and rounded per line.

Correctness invariants (the point of the module — do not remove):
- Numbers are allocated atomically via NumberAllocator (upsert + RETURNING),
  never MAX(number)+1. Allocation happens at issue, after the abort hook.
- Issued invoices are immutable; only drafts can be edited.
- Payments are idempotent (recordPaymentKey) so a redelivered webhook applies once.
- Void never deletes; paid invoices cannot be voided.

Agent rules:
- Keep use cases neutral; persistence behind InvoiceStore, numbering behind NumberAllocator.
- Never make real I/O in tests; inject the memory adapters.
- Ask before changing migrations, money mutations, or production deploy.
